findings.description: extract_verification_lifecycles: `root is None` -> bare `continue` (lines 1809-1811) is the one silent arm in an otherwise fully-instrumented function: a source-available transcript with no detectable root occurrence is skipped with no counter, no breadcrumb, no row annotation — registry-drift blindness indistinguishable from a genuinely launch-free corpus; zeroes the numerator at exit 0 with extraction_failure_count 0 ('unknown is not zero' broken at the numerator).

load_cloud_mappings silently returns {} on unreadable/corrupt registry (lines 993-1000, incl. non-dict branch), contradicting its own documented loud-degradation contract for non-absent failures; with a cloud snapshot present every cloud job then reads confirmed_ineligible while coverage reads available. TOCTOU-narrow today (load_registry just succeeded) but fails open on its documented contract and at full width for any future caller.

cleared: exporter gh failure modes all named
